body:
## Summary
- route the Giscus drawer loader and theme postMessage target through a configurable Giscus host
- default enabled comments to `https://comment-api.sepo.sh`
- document the new `GISCUS_APP_HOST` override

## Checks
- `npm run check`
- `GISCUS_APP_HOST=https://comment-api.sepo.sh npm run build`

Follow-up customization work can now happen in `self-evolving/comment-api` without requiring another GitHub App install.

comments.body: [vc]: #m/+2EjzHDmRf8/zUSqL3EqgUElPFBI6u9KBM1ZosjFI=: The latest updates on your projects. Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Canceled](https://vercel.com/static/status/canceled.svg) [Canceled](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/FK3zYkBK6E3AjoQz3qXS3Mtiqbb9) | [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-chore-use-sepo-com-f3b0e2-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 5, 2026 3:54pm |

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 49/51 | PARTIAL | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff touches only README and Giscus comment wiring across four files. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing `envValue` config path and existing `Comments` option plumbing in [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/quartz.ts#L101). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Adds one common-case env override, defaulting enabled comments to Sepo while preserving override behavior via `GISCUS_APP_HOST`. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 5/7 | Uses a distinct `appHost` option and `data-app-host`, but the host is not validated before browser runtime use in [`getGiscusHost`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L5). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README documents `GISCUS_APP_HOST` alongside existing Giscus env vars in [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/README.md#L150). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | `GISCUS_APP_HOST` names the general host capability rather than the Sepo-specific default. |
| communication/generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | The README keeps the change in the existing setup table without adding internal mechanics or migration detail. |

## Notes

- The main rubric strength is that the PR is narrow: config flows from `GISCUS_APP_HOST` into the component, then the inline script uses it for both `postMessage` target and `client.js` loading.
- Smallest useful follow-up: validate `GISCUS_APP_HOST` at config/build time, or document the accepted URL/host format so malformed values do not fail only when a reader opens the drawer.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `GISCUS_APP_HOST` is accepted as an arbitrary string in [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/quartz.ts#L101), then parsed in browser code with `new URL(...)` in [`comments.inline.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L5-L7). That partially misses the explicit-input validation preference because bad config can pass build checks and break at runtime.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #21 makes the Giscus service host configurable for the literature template.
It defaults enabled comments to `https://comment-api.sepo.sh` while preserving an override through `GISCUS_APP_HOST`.
The change threads that host from `quartz.ts` into the rendered comments container, then uses it for both `client.js` loading and theme `postMessage`.
This lets Sepo customize the hosted comment service without forcing downstream sites to install a different GitHub App.

## Review
Overall, this is a narrow and mostly clean change, with no blocking findings.
Two reviewer artifacts support a follow-up around validating/documenting the new host setting; one reviewer found no warning-level issues after checks and a build verification.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Validate `GISCUS_APP_HOST` input | WARNING | [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/quartz.ts#L101) accepts any string, then [`comments.inline.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L5-L7) normalizes it in the browser; missing-scheme or relative values silently become the page origin. |
| Document Sepo host dependency | WARNING | [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/2d94b54f5062ad65e15e83c2ea986cf75f5644b5/README.md#L150) says the default is “Sepo comments” but does not identify the Sepo-operated host, the absolute URL requirement, or the `https://giscus.app` escape hatch. |

## Progress
- The validation concern is already acknowledged in the current top-level rubrics review, so I did not duplicate it as an inline comment.
- Posted one new inline comment on `README.md:150` for the documentation gap.
- No existing inline comments or review threads were present before posting; no replies, minimizations, or thread resolutions were performed.
- Optional INFO notes about fallback defaults and tests were left out of required action items.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Validate `GISCUS_APP_HOST` input</summary>

**Cause:** `new URL(host, window.location.origin).origin` treats values like `comment-api.sepo.sh`, `""`, or `/foo` as relative to the literature site, so misconfiguration can produce a same-origin `/client.js` request instead of failing clearly.

**Candidate solutions:** Validate `GISCUS_APP_HOST` in `quartz.ts` as an absolute `http(s)` URL before passing it to `Comments`, or fail loudly in `getGiscusHost` when the configured value is not absolute.

**Comments:** Claude and the rubrics review both flagged this; Codex reviewed the same change and found no warning-level issue. The default value itself is valid, so this is minor rather than blocking.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Document Sepo host dependency</summary>

**Cause:** The README adds the env var but leaves “Sepo comments” underspecified for users deciding whether to depend on the Sepo-operated Giscus-compatible origin.

**Candidate solutions:** Expand the row or nearby prose to mention `https://comment-api.sepo.sh`, state that `GISCUS_APP_HOST=https://giscus.app` restores upstream Giscus behavior, and document that the value should be an absolute `https://` URL.

**Comments:** This is user-facing documentation, not a runtime defect, but it is a concrete branch change and safe for an automated fix.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The remaining issues are concrete, low-risk branch changes: validate the host input and expand the README entry.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Validate `GISCUS_APP_HOST` as an absolute `http(s)` URL before browser runtime use, or fail loudly for relative/missing-scheme input.
- [ ] Expand the `GISCUS_APP_HOST` README documentation to identify the Sepo default host, the upstream `https://giscus.app` escape hatch, and the absolute URL requirement.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Use Sepo-hosted Giscus service
